exercises.title: Timeline Analysis
exercises.description: Create a detailed timeline of the NIST PQC standardization process, identifying key milestones and the number of algorithms at each stage.
tasks: List all major milestones from 2016 to 2024
Track how many algorithms progressed through each round
Identify which algorithms were eliminated and why
Calculate the total duration of the process
exercises.expectedOutput: A comprehensive timeline document with dates, events, and algorithm counts
hints: Round 2 had 26, Round 3 had 15 (7 finalists + 8 alternates)

exercises.title: Security Category Mapping
exercises.description: Map the NIST security categories to real-world use cases and determine which category is appropriate for different applications.
tasks: Define what each security category (1-5) represents
List 3 use cases appropriate for Category 1
exercises.expectedOutput: A mapping table of security categories to use cases with justifications

exercises.title: Algorithm Comparison
exercises.description: Compare the three final NIST standards (ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA) in terms of their properties and appropriate use cases.
tasks: Compare the mathematical foundations of each
Compare key sizes and signature sizes
Identify the primary use case for each
Explain why NIST selected multiple signature schemes
hints: Consider lattice-based vs hash-based security assumptions

exercises.title: SIKE Break Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze the SIKE break and its implications for PQC deployment strategies.
tasks: Explain why this attack was unexpected
Discuss what this teaches about PQC deployment
Propose strategies to mitigate similar risks
exercises.expectedOutput: An analysis document with lessons learned and recommendations
hints: The attack was classical, not quantum
